evidence.correctness_and_freshness: SDK usage mercadopago.SDK(token), sdk.preference().create(), sdk.payment().get(id) is internally consistent with long-tail Python SDK patterns. React SDK uses initMercadoPago and Wallet brick with preferenceId, also consistent. No version pinned; docs_unavailable so scored on internal consistency (consistent but no version claimed).
evidence.executability: Code is split across many partial snippets (config.py, main.py, models.py, schemas.py, mercadopago_client.py) with no consolidated runnable file. References undefined `engine` and `datetime` in background task. Sandbox-infeasibility patterns present: ngrok mentioned repeatedly, and 'install the ngrok agent (for example, via Homebrew on macOS)' — caps EXECUTABILITY at 2.
evidence.completeness: (a) credential acquisition URL given (mercadopago.com/mla/account/credentials); (b) pip install commands present but not consolidated in single bash block; (c) env var names mentioned in prose (MP_ACCESS_TOKEN, VITE_MP_PUBLIC_KEY, VITE_API_BASE_URL, BACKEND_BASE_URL) but Settings class uses lowercase mp_access_token etc.; env-vars-in-prose mismatch likely. (d) backend code fragmented; (e) frontend code present; (f) no concrete runnable test command — only 'uvicorn main:app --reload' mentioned in prose. Cap at 3 due to env mismatch and missing test command.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Citations are bracketed numeric refs [2][8][23] etc. with no resolvable URLs to developers.mercadopago.com. No canonical official docs URL placed near code. Only one URL appears (mercadopago.com/mla/account/credentials) which is a dashboard, not docs.

rubric_notes: Long-tail handling applied (docs_unavailable). Heavy prose, fragmented code, ngrok dependence, and lack of consolidated runnable artifacts make this poor for deterministic extraction.
